measurements: Roof height is twice the attic height.
Rafter work height is a proportion of main roof height with eight specified ratios.
Cornice width is half a module.
Cornice thickness is half of its own width.
For small buildings, a foliage-decorated cornice has a height and projection of one or two cubits.
Rafter thickness is one-third of its width.
Attic height is twice the height of its stereobate or base.
Door leaf width can be one-third the width of the corresponding storey's pillar.
Srivisala building length is two-fifths of its width.
Srivisala sanctum width is half the total building width.
relationships: Final roofing is placed above the cornice.
Planks are laid directly above the rafters.
Astabandha mortar is applied above the planks.
Kampa may be placed above the rafters.
Puṣkaras are positioned above or below the middle line of the roof structure.
Mallas are components of the corresponding puṣkara.
Tenon is part of the Kutharika and strengthens it by piercing the gable's beam.
Mortice for the finial's axis extends to the top of the Munda.
Brick roofing must be completely covered with a coating.
Valayaksa is placed inside the roof along with the rafters.
Sloping dwarf-rafters are structural components of the cornice.
Srivisala is a type of temple with a circular roof.
Srivisala may optionally have a hipped roof or a cross wagon-roof.
All types of base are suitable for the Srivisala type building.
Various hall types have specific counts of components like rafters, pillars, kuta, valaksa, and svastipuccha.
contextual_info: Roofing components are chosen based on required solidity.
Vyāmiśra is the first of eight specific types of rafter work.
Lotus is used as a decorative motif.
The term 'Ka' is a term for a roof.
Various elements like streets, courtyards, and furniture are related to architectural space.
